title: manihot gracilis var. pronifolia
side_effects: Manihot gracilis var. pronifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea, due to its high concentration of anthraquinones. Improper use can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives, in some individuals. Furthermore, excessive consumption of Manihot gracilis var. pronifolia may cause kidney and liver damage due to its toxic compounds. In severe cases, it can even lead to more serious conditions, such as seizures and respiratory failure.
